title: region — outbound webhooks
description: Tenant admins can subscribe to region-lifecycle events via the webhook module (shared/webhooks, Wave 4). This blueprint declares the event types this module publishes. Payload shapes mirror the corresponding entries in `events.json` — the webhook module wraps them in the standard envelope. `tax_config.registration_number` is REDACTED in every outbound webhook payload — consumers who need it must re-fetch via the tenant-facing API.

event_types.description: New Region row created. Payload includes country_code, currency, timezone, locale, is_default, source. tax_config exposed WITHOUT registration_number.
Region mutated (name, timezone, locale, weekend_days, sort_order, subdivision_code, fiscal_year_start_month). Payload lists `changed_fields`.
Umbrella event fired alongside the specific transition event. Payload lists from_status + to_status + reason.
Region transitioned active → paused. New bookings blocked. Payload includes reason + expected_resume_at.
Region soft-deleted (archived). Payload includes reason + hard_delete_scheduled_for.
Archived Region transitioned back to active.
The default region flipped. Payload includes from_region_id + to_region_id. Financial-attribution-relevant event.

tax_config blob mutated. Payload includes `changed_fields` (dotted paths) but the actual before + after blobs are OMITTED for confidentiality — consumers must re-fetch. `registration_number` is NEVER in the payload.
Region soft-deleted OR hard-deleted (payload carries mode).
The tenant's default region was seeded on TenantProvisioned. Fires exactly once per tenant lifetime under normal operations.

notes: Every payload is signed with the tenant's webhook secret (HMAC-SHA256) per the shared webhook contract.
Payloads NEVER include the tax_config.registration_number (confidential-tier). Consumers who need it must re-fetch via the tenant-facing API + present `regions.view.tax_registration`.

The region.tax_config_updated event's payload EXCLUDES the full before + after snapshots by design — those live in the audit row and are read via the audit-scoped API. The webhook consumer sees only which fields changed, not the values.
